items.title: Validation and error recovery: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Disabling the submit button is enough to explain every invalid field.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Expose field-specific text errors and server validation; disabled submission alone does not identify what to fix.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Expose field-specific text errors and server validation; disabled submission alone does not identify what to fix.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Expose field-specific text errors and server validation; disabled submission alone does not identify what to fix.

items.title: Validation and error recovery: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Combine native constraints and domain validation with persistent instructions and focus-aware errors. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating persistent accessible validation error and recovery; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.

answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded react artifact for persistent accessible validation error and recovery. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
